title: Download the Hitek Work Desktop App
subtitle: Install the Desktop App on employees' machines to start recording time and activity. Choose the build that matches your operating system.

windows.label: Windows
windows.hint: .exe file — works on most Windows 10/11 machines.

macIntel.label: macOS (Intel)
macIntel.hint: Choose this if your Mac has an Intel chip (usually models before 2020).

macSilicon.label: macOS (Apple Silicon)
macSilicon.hint: Choose this if your Mac has an Apple M1/M2/M3 chip (usually models from 2020 on).

linux.label: Linux
linux.hint: .AppImage file — runs on most 64-bit Linux distributions.

instructions.title: Install & bypass warnings
instructions.intro: Apps published by Hitek are not code-signed with a publisher certificate, so your operating system may show a warning on first install. This is expected — follow the steps below to continue.

windows.title: Windows
steps: Open the downloaded .exe file. If a "Windows protected your PC" window (SmartScreen) appears, click "More info".
Click "Run anyway" to continue the installation.
Follow the installer to completion, then sign in with the account provided.

mac.title: macOS (Intel & Apple Silicon)
steps: Open the downloaded .dmg file and drag Hitek Work into the Applications folder.
Open the app for the first time; if macOS says it "cannot be opened because the developer cannot be verified", go to System Settings > Privacy & Security.
Scroll to the Security section, click "Open Anyway" next to the warning about Hitek Work, then confirm.

linux.title: Linux
steps: After downloading the .AppImage file, make it executable: right-click > Properties > Permissions > check "Allow executing file as program" (or run chmod +x your-file.AppImage).
Double-click to open, or run ./your-file.AppImage from a terminal.
Sign in with the account provided to get started.
